input_text: Transformers use attention to compare every token with every other token in a sequence. This makes it easier to capture long range relationships than a simple recurrent model.
target_text: Transformers use attention to model long range token relationships.

input_text: An encoder reads the full input sequence at once and produces contextual representations. Encoder-only models are often used for classification, tagging, retrieval, and embedding tasks.
target_text: Encoders produce contextual input representations for understanding tasks.

input_text: A decoder generates tokens one at a time while masking future positions. Decoder-only models are commonly used for text completion and chat style generation.
target_text: Decoders generate masked next tokens for text generation.

input_text: An encoder-decoder model first encodes the source text and then decodes the target text. This design is useful for translation, summarization, and other sequence-to-sequence tasks.
target_text: Encoder-decoder models support sequence-to-sequence generation.

input_text: Teacher forcing trains a decoder by providing the previous gold target token as input. At inference time the model must use its own previously generated tokens instead.
target_text: Teacher forcing uses gold previous tokens during decoder training.

input_text: Positional information is needed because attention alone does not know token order. Models add learned or fixed position signals to token embeddings.
target_text: Position signals help attention understand token order.

input_text: Multi-head attention lets a model learn several attention patterns in parallel. Some heads may focus on syntax while others capture semantic or positional cues.
target_text: Multi-head attention learns several parallel attention patterns.

input_text: The feed-forward block applies the same small neural network to each token representation. It adds non-linear transformations after attention mixing.
target_text: Feed-forward blocks transform each token after attention.

input_text: During inference, greedy decoding always picks the most likely next token. Beam search keeps several candidate sequences and often produces stronger outputs.
target_text: Beam search keeps multiple candidates during generation.

input_text: Padding tokens should be masked so the model does not treat them as real content. Padding masks are especially important when examples in a batch have different lengths.
target_text: Padding masks prevent attention to padded positions.

target_text: Cross-attention lets a decoder attend to encoder outputs while generating a target sequence.
